page: Amazon Web Services c8id.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8). The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

For local storage, the c8id.8xlarge includes 1900 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. This local storage configuration is bundled directly with the instance, providing high-speed, low-latency data access. Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 15 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance profiles across multiple domains. The instance achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core testing and bzip3 single-threaded decompression. It also demonstrates strong performance in multi-core operations, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 18417.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 51792.67. Large-block read memory bandwidth is strong at 180.41 GB/sec, while write bandwidth and database operations fall into the average tier. LLM inference benchmarks show strong performance, processing 220.66 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8id.8xlarge offers high compute density and fast DDR5 memory, making it a viable option for compute-heavy workloads that require high-speed local NVMe storage without the overhead of external network-attached storage. The presence of AMX instructions enhances its efficiency for CPU-based machine learning inference. This instance is well-suited for workloads such as high-performance web serving, software compilation, image processing, text processing, and local database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix operations. In benchmarks, the instance delivers top-tier performance in single-core processing, image manipulation, and text processing, alongside strong multi-core and LLM inference capabilities. With a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio and high-speed local storage, the c8id.8xlarge is designed for compute-intensive workloads, including software compilation, web serving, and local database operations that benefit from high-speed temporary storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and 1900 GB NVMe SSD local storage, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-frequency compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and integrated high-speed NVMe local storage.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
64.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
1900 GB of integrated NVMe SSD local instance storage
Top-tier single-core CPU, image processing, and text processing benchmark performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix computations
Optimized for compute-heavy workloads, software compilation, and database operations
